expectations.text: Output includes a coverage matrix mapping design elements to test cases
expectations.evidence: coverage_matrix.md exists with detailed tables across 4 strategies: Requirement-Based (17 tests), Equivalence Class Partitioning (28 tests), Boundary Value Analysis (12 tests), Error Handling & Fault Injection (15 tests). Maps design behavior steps to specific test case names.

expectations.text: Every sequential behavior step's on_failure path has a dedicated test (invalid_sync, unsupported_version, length_mismatch, checksum_failed, unknown_type)
expectations.evidence: invalid_sync: test_rejects_invalid_first_sync_byte and test_rejects_invalid_second_sync_byte assert ErrorCode.INVALID_SYNC. unsupported_version: test_rejects_unsupported_version_zero and test_rejects_unsupported_version_two assert ErrorCode.UNSUPPORTED_VERSION. length_mismatch: test_rejects_payload_declared_longer_than_actual and test_rejects_payload_actual_longer_than_declared assert ErrorCode.LENGTH_MISMATCH. checksum_failed: test_rejects_corrupted_checksum and test_rejects_checksum_off_by_one assert ErrorCode.CHECKSUM_FAILED. unknown_type: test_rejects_unknown_message_type asserts ErrorCode.UNKNOWN_TYPE.

expectations.text: Every error_handling entry (null/empty raw_data, too-short raw_data) has a dedicated test
expectations.evidence: Null input: test_rejects_null_input asserts NullPointerException. Empty input: test_rejects_empty_input asserts ErrorCode.INVALID_SYNC. Too-short input: test_rejects_input_below_minimum_size (11 bytes) asserts ErrorCode.LENGTH_MISMATCH. Single-byte input: test_rejects_single_byte_input also covers too-short.

expectations.text: Boundary values tested for raw_data length (4 bytes minimum, 1024 bytes maximum, 3 bytes too short, 1025 bytes too long)
expectations.evidence: Minimum tested is 12 bytes (HEADER_SIZE + CHECKSUM_SIZE) via test_accepts_minimum_size_message. Maximum 1024 bytes tested via test_accepts_maximum_size_message. Too short tested at 11 bytes via test_rejects_input_below_minimum_size. Too long tested at 1025 bytes via test_rejects_input_exceeding_maximum. However, the assertion requires specific values of 4 bytes minimum and 3 bytes too short. The test uses 12 bytes as minimum and 11 bytes as too-short, not 4 and 3. The design minimum appears to be 12 (header+checksum), not 4. Strict reading: the specific values 4 and 3 are not tested.

expectations.text: Union return type handled: tests assert on specific Success or Error variant fields, not just non-null
expectations.evidence: Success variant: tests assert on result.isSuccess() and then access result.getMessageType(), result.getSequenceNumber(), result.getPayload() with specific value assertions (e.g., assertEquals(0x42, result.getMessageType())). Error variant: tests assert on result.isError() and then access result.getErrorCode() with specific enum values and result.getByteOffset() with specific positions.

expectations.text: No test uses only 'assertDoesNotThrow' or 'assertNotNull' as its sole assertion
expectations.evidence: All tests include specific assertions on result.isSuccess()/result.isError() combined with field-level assertions (assertEquals on error codes, message types, sequence numbers, byte offsets). No test uses only assertDoesNotThrow or assertNotNull as its sole assertion.

expectations.text: Test names describe the scenario being tested, not just the method name
expectations.evidence: Test names use @DisplayName annotations describing scenarios: 'rejects invalid first sync byte', 'accepts supported version (1)', 'rejects message where declared payload length exceeds actual data', 'rejects unknown message type'. Method names also describe scenarios: test_rejects_invalid_first_sync_byte, test_accepts_valid_sync_bytes.

expectations.text: Happy path test: valid message parsed correctly with verified payload fields
expectations.evidence: test_parses_payload_fields attempts this but the helper method validMessageWithPayload is a stub that just delegates to validMessageWithMessageType without actually encoding payload fields. The assertion assertTrue(result.getPayload().containsKey('key1')) would not actually work as no keys are encoded in the payload bytes. The test cannot meaningfully verify parsed payload field values. There is no complete happy-path test that constructs a real message and verifies all extracted fields (message type, sequence number, AND payload content with actual values).
